pattern: Rebuilding every node's value from its cousins takes a two-pass level-order sweep: first tally each level's total and each parent's own children sum, then a node's cousins sum is simply the level total minus whatever its own parent contributed.
hints: The root always becomes 0 since it has no cousins at all; think level by level below it.
For every level, work out the level's grand total value and, separately, how much each parent's own pair of children contributes.
A node's new value is its level's grand total minus its own parent's children-sum — siblings share this same new value since they share a parent.

edge_inputs.description: left-skewed chain, every node a lone child
right-skewed chain, every node a lone child
one branch missing entirely at depth 2
root with a single lone left child only

stress.description: random level-order tree with random gaps, values 1..10000
stress.note: level-order array with trailing nulls trimmed keeps it a valid wire-form tree
